title: Headings, Paragraphs & Text
summary: Headings label sections and come in levels from h1 to h6.
h1 is the most important, h6 the least.
Normal blocks of text go inside the p element.
Elements like strong and em give words extra meaning.

sections.text: Most of a web page is just text, but not all text is equal. Some of it is a title, some is a paragraph, and some needs to stand out. HTML has an element for each.

sections.title: Headings
sections.text: Headings label the sections of a page. They go from h1 down to h6. h1 is the biggest and most important, usually the page title. The smaller numbers mark smaller subsections. You use them in order so the page has a clear outline.

sections.title: Paragraphs and Text Meaning
sections.text: Normal blocks of text go inside a p element, short for paragraph. To give words extra meaning you can use strong for something important, or em for emphasis. These change the meaning, not only the look.

media.alt: A page outline showing h1, h2, and h3 nested in order

sections.title: A Book's Table of Contents
sections.text: Headings are like the chapter and section titles in a book. h1 is the book title, h2 is a chapter, h3 is a section inside that chapter. Read only the headings and you still understand the shape of the whole thing.

media.alt: A book opening to a table of contents with nested titles

sections.title: Using Text Elements Well
items: Use one h1 for the main title of the page.
Use h2 for the main sections under it.
Use h3 and below for smaller subsections, in order.
Put normal text inside p elements.
Use strong or em to mark words that carry extra meaning.

sections.text: Skipping heading levels, like jumping from h1 straight to h4, can confuse screen readers. Going in order keeps the page easy to follow.

terms.definition: A label for a section of the page.
The most important heading, usually the page title.
The element that holds a normal block of text.

questions.text: Which heading is the most important?
questions.explanation: h1 is the most important heading, usually used for the page title.

questions.text: What element holds a normal block of text?
questions.explanation: The p element, short for paragraph, holds a normal block of text.

options: So the page has a clear outline screen readers can follow
questions.explanation: Headings in order create a clear outline that screen readers and people can follow.
